purpose: Scope a metadata repository or metamodel across architecture-framework rows and columns.
required_context: columns in scope: data, activities, locations, people, time/events, motivation
rows in scope: business owner, architect, designer, builder, functioning system
ask_if_missing: Which governance or operational decisions must this metadata support?
Which mappings must be traceable from business meaning to implementation?
assume_if_low_risk: start with business-owner and architect rows before physical inventory
treat table/column catalog as technical metadata, not the whole repository

branches.criteria: business glossary, semantic reconciliation, or concept ownership is the main need
branches.recommendation: prioritize Data column Row Two and Row Three: terms, concepts, semantic communities, fact types, entity classes, attributes, domains, and stewardship

branches.criteria: lineage from policies/rules into controls and data-quality evidence is the main need
branches.recommendation: prioritize Motivation column mappings from policy to business rule to system constraint to enforcement and measurement

branches.criteria: catalog must support development and operations across systems
branches.recommendation: include designer/builder/functioning-system rows for schemas, program modules, interfaces, deployments, owners, logs, and freshness checks

branches.criteria: event-driven or workflow architecture is central
branches.recommendation: include Activities and Events columns, mapping function/process/event type to system process, program event, state change, and logs

failure_modes: metadata repository is only a database dictionary and cannot answer business meaning questions
glossary terms are not scoped by semantic community or context
business rules are prose with no link to concepts, constraints, implementation, or evidence
production inventories are stale because ownership and freshness are undefined
business and technical artifacts are merged without explicit mapping relationships
output_checks: separates business metadata, technical metadata, and meta-metadata
includes validation/freshness checks for the repository itself
